summary: The article supports a bounded claim that a coordinated context-engineering workflow may improve multi-agent code-assistant performance, but the mechanism and evidence remain only lightly specified in the provided material.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The system outperforms existing frameworks on accuracy and reliability.
bounded_claims.bounds: The provided context says performance was compared against existing frameworks, but it does not include benchmark design, metrics, effect sizes, or replication details.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The workflow demonstrates stronger performance on real-world codebases.
bounded_claims.bounds: This claim is presented in the findings and draft, but the excerpt does not provide dataset scope, task composition, or failure cases, so the result should be treated as bounded rather than fully established.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The paper establishes why the workflow works beyond reporting better outcomes.
bounded_claims.bounds: No detailed causal account is available in the provided material beyond the general claim that coordinated context engineering improves performance.
